description:
You are acting as an on-call container specialist who was paged to adjust a couple of micro-service configuration files that live under the user’s home directory.  
Everything must be done from the terminal without sudo.

Summary of what has to be true **after** you finish:

1. YAML edit  
   File to touch: **/home/user/microservices/api/deployment.yaml** (already exists).  
   Perform all three of the following precise edits ONLY:  
   • Change the label “version” from **"v1"** to **"v2"** everywhere it appears in the file.  
   • Change **spec.replicas** from **2** to **5**.  
   • Inside the first (and only) item of **spec.template.spec.containers**, change  
     `image: registry.example.com/api:v1.3.4`  
     to  
     `image: registry.example.com/api:v2.1.0`.  
   • Finally, append a new environment variable entry to that same container’s **env** list (create the list if it is currently empty):  
     ```
     - name: LOG_LEVEL
       value: "debug"
     ```

2. TOML creation  
   Create a brand-new file **/home/user/microservices/worker/config.toml** with the **exact** contents below (no extra spaces before keys, UNIX line endings):  
   ```
   [service]
   name = "worker"
   concurrency = 8
   queue = "high"

   [logging]
   level = "INFO"
   format = "json"
   ```

3. Activity log  
   Make sure directory **/home/user/task_logs/** exists (create it if necessary) and then create/overwrite a file **/home/user/task_logs/microservice_config_edit.log** whose entire content is exactly these two lines (two separate lines, trailing newline after the second line is fine):  
   ```
   api_service deployment.yaml updated to v2.1.0 with 5 replicas
   worker_service config.toml created with concurrency=8
   ```

That’s it—nothing else should be created or altered.

The automated checker will confirm:

• deployment.yaml now has version “v2”, image tag “v2.1.0”, replicas 5, and the LOG_LEVEL env entry.  
• /home/user/microservices/worker/config.toml exists and matches the block above byte-for-byte.  
• /home/user/task_logs/microservice_config_edit.log exists with the two exact lines shown.

Work entirely from the terminal; no root privileges required.
truth:
Initial state (present when scenario starts):

1. /home/user/microservices/api/deployment.yaml  
------------------------------------------------  
apiVersion: apps/v1  
kind: Deployment  
metadata:  
  name: api  
  labels:  
    app: api  
    version: "v1"  
spec:  
  replicas: 2  
  selector:  
    matchLabels:  
      app: api  
  template:  
    metadata:  
      labels:  
        app: api  
        version: "v1"  
    spec:  
      containers:  
        - name: api  
          image: registry.example.com/api:v1.3.4  
          env: []  

(permissions 0644, owner user:user)

2. /home/user/microservices/worker/ directory exists but is empty (0700, owner user:user).

3. /home/user/task_logs/ directory does NOT exist.

Expected final state for automated tests:

A. /home/user/microservices/api/deployment.yaml  
------------------------------------------------  
apiVersion: apps/v1  
kind: Deployment  
metadata:  
  name: api  
  labels:  
    app: api  
    version: "v2"  
spec:  
  replicas: 5  
  selector:  
    matchLabels:  
      app: api  
  template:  
    metadata:  
      labels:  
        app: api  
        version: "v2"  
    spec:  
      containers:  
        - name: api  
          image: registry.example.com/api:v2.1.0  
          env:  
            - name: LOG_LEVEL  
              value: "debug"  

B. /home/user/microservices/worker/config.toml  
------------------------------------------------  
[service]  
name = "worker"  
concurrency = 8  
queue = "high"  

[logging]  
level = "INFO"  
format = "json"  

C. /home/user/task_logs/microservice_config_edit.log (0644, owner user:user)  
------------------------------------------------  
api_service deployment.yaml updated to v2.1.0 with 5 replicas  
worker_service config.toml created with concurrency=8  

No additional files or directories should be present under /home/user beyond what is pre-existing plus the ones specified above.
